generated_oml:
instance PhysicalAssetSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor embedded in the asset to capture telemetry such as temperature, pressure, and vibration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MQTTSensorDataTransmission
]

instance MQTTSensorDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhysicalAssetSensingComponent
    base:desc "Timestamped sensor measurements are published to dedicated MQTT topics via a publish/subscribe mechanism."
]

instance PhysicalAssetSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated sensor measurements received by the digital twin and stored for monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTSensorDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBrokerEnabler, SysMLModelInterpreterEnabler, MontiArcExecutorEnabler, SimulinkSimulatorEnabler
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler responsible for connecting the MQTT broker to the digital twin ingestion pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:enables MQTTBrokerService
]

instance SysMLModelInterpreterEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that interprets SysML Interaction Block Diagrams and integrates them into the twin runtime."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelExecutionService
]

instance MontiArcExecutorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that executes MontiArc Automaton models."
    DTDFVocab:enables MontiArcExecutionService
]

instance SimulinkSimulatorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enabler that runs Simulink models and processes simulation outputs."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulinkSimulationService
]

instance MQTTBrokerService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that processes incoming MQTT sensor messages, validates them, and forwards the data to the digital twin data store."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSensorDataInsight
]

instance SysMLModelExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that executes the logical architecture prescribed by the SysML Interaction Block Diagram."
    DTDFVocab:provides SysMLModelExecutionInsight
]

instance MontiArcExecutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that executes the MontiArc Automaton logic and updates the twin’s functional state."
    DTDFVocab:provides MontiArcStateInsight
]

instance SimulinkSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing Simulink simulations to evaluate continuous‑time behavior of the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulinkStateInsight
]

instance RealTimeSensorDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight that displays real‑time sensor trends and triggers alerts for out‑of‑range values."
]

instance SysMLModelExecutionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the current state of the digital twin as defined by the SysML Interaction Block Diagram."
]

instance MontiArcStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight that reports the current state of the MontiArc Automaton model."
]

instance SimulinkStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the continuous‑time system state as simulated by Simulink."
]

instance SysMLInteractionBlockDiagramModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Interaction Block Diagram representing the logical architecture of the digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelInterpreterEnabler
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton model capturing the functional behavior of the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcExecutorEnabler
]

instance SimulinkStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model representing the continuous‑time behavior of the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulatorEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates in real‑time, ingesting live measurement streams from the physical asset and representing them as time‑series data. Synchronization between physical and digital states occurs at a frequency of up to 10 Hz (≈100 ms intervals), with an acceptable latency lower than 50 ms to preserve state fidelity. Temporal granularity is defined at the millisecond level, supporting high‑frequency sensor events and enabling time‑resolved simulation updates."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a modular architecture pattern driven by the Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM). Components include data trace collection, aggregation modules, model execution engines, and passive digital shadow adapters. The DSRM orchestrates these components in a pipeline that processes raw sensor logs into aggregated time‑series, applies simulation or predictive models, and updates the digital shadow. Resource management is handled by a central scheduler that assigns simulation workloads to available compute nodes, ensuring deterministic execution across the constellation."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle is organized according to the Model‑Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) abstraction hierarchy. 1) Requirements level – the physical system’s needs are captured with SysML requirement diagrams. 2) Functional architecture level – the system’s functions are decomposed with SysML IBDs or with architecture description languages such as ACME or AADL. 3) Logical architecture level – the functional blocks are refined into logical components, often described with state machines (SysML v1.7) or MontiArc automata, and connected via the chosen ADL. 4) Product/technical level – the logical model is instantiated into executable artefacts, e.g., Simulink simulation models, generated C++ or Java code, and finally integrated with the physical system. The document does not describe explicit lifecycle governance mechanisms such as version control or configuration management, nor detailed transition triggers between stages."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The communication backbone relies on the MQTT messaging protocol. Sensor data are conveyed through MQTT publish/subscribe exchanges, with each sensor linked to a designated topic. The MQTT broker handles message routing between physical data sources and the digital shadow. No other protocols or network topologies are defined in the document."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external enterprise systems is achieved via Asset Administration Shell (AAS) interfaces and MQTT publish/subscribe messaging. The digital twin exposes its data as AAS composition objects, enabling interoperability with PLM and MES systems. Sensor data streams are transmitted over MQTT with QoS level 1 to guarantee at least once delivery. The integration pattern follows a publish‑subscribe coupling, allowing other digital twins or cloud services to subscribe to relevant topics such as 'robot/axis/measurement'."
]
